rulings.Case Title: EST. OF SARE KING WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Sell Real Property And To Enter Into Exclusive Right-To- Sell Agreements For 1224-1226 Stockton Street, San Francisco, California 94133
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/05/2022 to be heard with the related status petition. The following issues e-mailed to counsel on 02/14/2022 remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The proof of service attached to the DE-120 form does not list Heath Langle, though identified in paragraph 13 of the petition as a person who requested special notice. 3) The petition and DE-120 form must refer to the petitioner's capacity as administrator "with will annexed". 4) Re paragraph 4 of the petition: i) Special letters were extended on 09/19/2018. ii) Letters were issued on 02/26/2019. iii) Item 1b, rather than item 2a, of letters had to be checked. An ex parte petition for an order authorizing the issuance of amended letters must be filed to correctly identify the petitioner's capacity as administrator with will annexed. 5) Re paragraph 6: The APN must be included in the legal description. 6) A certification required by Probate Code section 8800(d) re compliance with section 480 of the Revenue & Taxation Code must be filed with respect to the Stockton property. An appearance may be required on 07/05/2022 on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress if the issues are not fully addressed.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SANDY CAMERON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Issues posted for the 3/23/22 have not yet been addressed: The proof of service has dates that cannot be true; paragraphs 6 and 7 of the petition seem to have conflicting figures re the conservator's compensation and are inconsistent with the prayer; and the bank statements for the end of the 2nd and 3rd accounting periods have not yet been filed. In addition, a closer review of the accountings revealed further issues. The Summary of Account for the actual 2nd accounting period, shown on page 3 of the petition, differs from the actual accounting summary starting value and does not balance. The Wells Fargo account at the end of the first accounting was $3,460.97, not rounded up. Why was there "misc personal property" not inventoried (but sold on 3/18/16)? Please file documents supporting the sale of the silver (all at a loss) and to verify the auction house commissions. It appears there was a duplicate payment to Lavinia's Home on 6/6/16. Was the $1,400 deposit for placement refunded when the conservatee moved from there? There were also two April rent payments and two July rent payments to Bancroft Health Care in 2018 and 2019. Why were there additional amounts or payments to "catch up" made to Bancroft Health Care in 2020 but no August 2021 rent? The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth in a conservatorship accounting. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] None of the three accountings reflects any liabilities, although the 3rd and 4th accountings show additional fees paid to the conservator and to her attorney. Fees paid during the 3rd accounting period (10/10/17 - 3/7/18) also need further clarification, as they were apparently varying percentages of the sale of property...? Why were there deposits shown as receipts on 11/2/17 ($400) and 3/12/18 ($158.68) for "sale of jewelry" when all of it was reportedly sold on 3/18/18 at a loss of $4,788? The Court's records show the last post-move notice filed on 9/14/17, when the conservatee was moved to Parkview Healthcare Center in Hayward. The accounting reflects payments to Lavinia's Home and Bancroft Health Care. The Court Investigator reported that she was told the conservator rarely visited the conservatee even prior to the pandemic. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LUIS URIBE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. Original financial statements have not been filed. No proposed order has been submitted. The funds reported to be on hand in paragraph 17 of the petition and paragraph 6 of the prayer are not the funds shown in the accounting. Clearly, the funds may be bought and sold, so they are not effectively blocked at all. (Is Saylor Insurance and Financial Services the same as Lincoln Financial or is the Lincoln National Life Insurance annuity separately held?) Three parties billed to appear on 9/8/20? Only one attorney appeared on that date. What was filed via NorCal Courier on 6/4/18? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/1/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Block amounts are not acceptable. 2) Please address whether the $1,995.00 preneed funeral expenses are a conservatorship asset that should be included in the accounting. 3) Please explain whether Paul L. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA MARGARITA ESPINOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. Neither a petition for final distribution nor the required petition for approval of status report has been filed, pursuant to Probate Code 12200. Counsel has not complied and instead of filing a petition and paying the filing fee counsel filed a status statement not signed or verified by the administrators. Petitions for Approval of Status Report need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Petitions need to include all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELLIOT S EISENBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amended petition was just filed on 5/23/22, but not actually efiled until 5/26/22, when it was first available for viewing. No courtesy copies of the amended petition or supporting papers were received by the assigned examiner by 5/27/22 (or even as of 6/1/22). Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers filed to address issues posted for the 5/10/22 hearing, as well as a proof of service showing at least 15 days' notice of the hearing of the amended petition on 7/5/22, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Still NO COURTESY COPY of the petition or of the proof of service filed on 3/8/22 or of the Declaration filed on 4/5/22 or of the proof of service filed on 5/23/22 was received by the assigned examiner by 5/27/22 deadline (or even as of 6/1/22). [LRSF 14.18 D.] A courtesy copy of the PETITION and any other papers filed on or after 4/5/22 must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/30/22. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENNIE L. GAINES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The DATE OF DEATH of the pre-deceased husband was not set forth. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Was he a former husband, as in, the couple was divorced? Then, item 5a(2)(b) of the petition would be incorrect. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is still incomplete at item 2. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/22 . (An amendment to a pleading is NOT the same as a supplement. [CRC 7.3.] An amendment requires notice. A supplement ordinarily does not. [CRC 7.53.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
